pattern: Flood-fill each 4-connected component of positive cells, accumulate its summed value, and count the components whose total is divisible by k.
hints: Land is any positive cell; zeros are water. Two land cells belong to the same island when connected horizontally or vertically.
Walk the grid; when you find an unvisited land cell, explore its whole connected component while summing the values you visit.
After computing each island's total value, increment the answer when that total modulo k is zero.

edge_inputs.description: six single-cell islands all divisible by 3
single land cell divisible by k
single land cell not divisible by k
one large island whose total is divisible but individual cells are not
diagonal-only contact does not connect cells

stress.description: random grid of small values and zeros so many islands form with varied totals
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(30, size)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(30, size)))
    grid = [[(rng.randint(1, 9) if rng.random() < 0.55 else 0) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 10)
    return [grid, k]
stress.note: iterative flood fill avoids recursion limits
